summary: Kalshi list endpoints wrap rows in a resource-named key alongside a `cursor` field (e.g. {"fills":[...],"cursor":"..."}); --all must extract that named array, follow `cursor`, and return [] (never null) when empty.
reason: The generator's paginator looked for a generic wrapper and next-page field Kalshi does not send, so every paginated read returned {"results": null}; the same read path also swallowed a 409 on a GET as an idempotent no-op instead of surfacing it.
validated_outcome: internal/cli/paginated_get_test.go exercises envelope cursor-following, the sticky-cursor guard, empty-set null-to-[] conversion, and 409-on-GET surfacing.
